大阪直美在大阪出生，母親是日本人，父親是美國人，依據採「屬人主義」的日本國籍法，大阪直美是「日本人」本無疑義。但國籍法第11條第1節中又寫道：「日本國民若是取得他國國籍，將自動失去原有之日本國籍」。而根據亞洲時報的報導，任何擁有雙重國籍的日本人，都必須在20-22歲這兩年時間內，決定要要成為哪一個國家的國民，否則一旦年滿22歲，日本政府即依法有權剝奪其日本國籍。

「日本政府通常不會積極處理國籍問題，也很少認真執行這項規定，」天普大學歷史系教授Jeff Kingston說。

Newly crowned Australian Open champion and newly anointed world No.1 Naomi Osaka overlooked one detail as she came to grips with new status after her victory over Petra Kvitova.

"I forgot to smile," she said. "I was told to smile and I didn't. I was panicking."

Osaka forfeited three match points in the second set before recovering to win an epic final in three. In tears at the end of the second set, she said she was able to recover her composure quickly because she can "disassociate" her feelings.

"You know how some people get worked up about things?" she said. "That's a very human thing to do. Sometimes I don't want to waste my energy doing stuff like that. I think about this on the court, too. In the third set today, I literally just tried to turn off all my feelings.

"I felt kinda hollow. I was a robot, sort of. I was just executing my orders. I just did what I've been practising my whole life in a way. I didn't waste any energy reacting too much."
